recalls.Description: Stoney Games is recalling the Kluster Fun Tabletop Magnet Chess Games, which contain small, loose magnets measuring approximately .5 to one inch wide and .3 to .4 inches high. These games are packaged in a black box featuring the name 'Kluster' and include around 24 magnets, an orange string, an instruction manual, and a black storage pouch labeled 'Kluster.' Some games were also available in a white pouch with gameplay instructions on the back.
recalls.Title: Stoney Games Issues Recall for Kluster Magnet Chess Games Due to Serious Injury Risk from Magnet Ingestion
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Stoney Games at 800-362-0977 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email klusterrecall@gmail.com, or visit www.klustermagnets.com/recall. You can also find more information by going to www.klustermagnets.com and clicking 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Kluster Fun Tabletop Magnet Chess Games

Images.Caption: Recalled Kluster Fun Tabletop Magnet Chess Game (Sold in White Pouch)
Recalled Kluster Fun Tabletop Magnet Chess Game (Back of Pouch with Gameplay Instructions)
Recalled Kluster Fun Tabletop Magnet Chess Game (Game Contents Sold in Black Box)

Retailers.Name: Available at independent game stores across the country, as well as online at Klustermagnets.com, Amazon.com, and Etsy.com from October 2018 to September 2025, priced between $15 and $20.

Importers.Name: Stoney Games, LLC of Bexley, Ohio

Hazards.Name: The recalled games pose a serious ingestion hazard as they contain loose high-powered magnets that fit within the CPSC's small parts cylinder. If swallowed, these magnets can attract each other or other metal objects, leading to potential intestinal perforations, twisting, blockage, blood poisoning, or even death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled magnet games immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Stoney Games for guidance on safe disposal and to obtain replacement magnets that do not pose a choking hazard.

recalls.metaDescription: Stoney Games recalls Kluster Magnet Chess Games due to serious ingestion risks. Learn how to safely dispose of the product and get replacements.

recalls.Description: The recall affects a set of 10 flexible stick figures available in various colors including purple, black, red, orange, pink, yellow, bright blue, dark blue, white, and green. Each figure, packaged in clear plastic labeled 'Grtard,' features four small magnets located in their hands and feet. These toys are designed in both solid and transparent styles.
recalls.Title: Grtard Magnetic Stick Figure Toy Sets Recalled Due to Serious Injury Risks from Magnet Ingestion
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, email Grtardrecall@outlook.com.

Products.Name: Grtard Magnetic Stick Figure Toy Set

Images.Caption: Recalled Grtard Magnet Men Stick Figure Toy Set

Retailers.Name: Available online at Walmart.com from May 2025 to November 2025 for approximately $12.

Distributors.Name: Shanghaijinlianwangluokejiyouxiangongsi (Shanghai Jinlian Network Technology Co., Ltd.), dba Grtard, of China

Hazards.Name: The toy sets contain loose magnets, violating safety standards and posing a serious ingestion risk to children. If swallowed, these powerful magnets can attract each other or metal objects, leading to severe internal injuries such as perforations, twisting, or blockages in the digestive tract, which can result in blood poisoning or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are urged to immediately stop using the recalled stick figures and keep them away from children. To obtain a full refund, contact Grtard and provide a photo of the disposed product via email at Grtardrecall@outlook.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Grtard recalls magnetic stick figure toy sets due to serious ingestion hazards. Learn how to get a refund and keep children safe.

recalls.Description: A recall has been issued for magnetic chess games that feature small, loose magnets capable of detaching due to their strong magnetic force. These games come packaged in a blue box labeled with terms like "Magnetic", "Magnetic Chess Piece", and "Intelligence Strategy Game". Each set includes 20 loose magnets, a yellow string, gameplay instructions, and a plastic storage bag.
recalls.Title: Magnetic Chess Games Recalled Due to Serious Injury Risks from Magnet Ingestion
recalls.ConsumerContact: For inquiries, contact Kaiwenshangpin via email at Kaiwenshangpinrecall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Magnetic Chess Game
Recalled Magnetic Chess Game (front of box)
Recalled Magnetic Chess Game (back of box)
Recalled Magnetic Chess Game (game contents)

Importers.Name: Yiwushixiuxiangfushiyouxiangongsi ("Yiwu Xiu Xiang Garment Co., Ltd.") dba Kaiwenshangpin, of China

Hazards.Name: The magnetic chess games are a safety risk as they contain loose magnets that can be ingested by children, leading to severe health issues. If swallowed, these magnets can attract each other or other metal objects, potentially causing intestinal blockages, perforations, blood poisoning, or even death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must immediately stop using the recalled magnetic chess games, keep them away from children, and dispose of them. To obtain a full refund, consumers should email a photo of the disposed game to Kaiwenshangpinrecall@outlook.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of magnetic chess games due to ingestion hazards. Immediate action required for safety. Contact for refunds.

recalls.Description: A recall has been issued for magnetic chess games that pose a significant risk of injury or death due to loose magnets. These games feature a festive Christmas design, including 20 magnets, a red string, instructions, and a red drawstring storage bag. The packaging displays holiday imagery such as Santa Claus, snowmen, and a Christmas tree, with 'Merry Christmas' on the front and 'Magnetic' on the back.
recalls.Title: Magnetic Chess Games Recalled for Serious Injury Risk from Magnet Ingestion; Sold by Yiruikeji2024 on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: Contact Yiruikeji2024 via email at yirui2024@yeah.net.

Importers.Name: Shenzhenshiyiruikejiyouxiangongsi (Shenzhenshi Yirui Technology Co., Ltd.), dba Yiruikeji2024, of China

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to loose magnets that can cause severe internal injuries.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the product immediately, keep it away from children, and contact Yiruikeji2024 for a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of magnetic chess games due to ingestion risks. Contact Yiruikeji2024 for refunds and safety information.

recalls.Description: Huaker has issued a recall for their Magnetic Balls and Rods sets, which consist of 88 pieces packaged in a plastic box with a handle. The box is labeled with 'Magnetic Sticks' and 'Model:20A-13'. These magnetic toys do not have any additional identifying marks. The sets pose a serious choking hazard for children under three years of age due to the presence of small balls, violating safety standards for toys.
recalls.Title: Huaker Magnetic Balls and Rods Sets Recalled for Choking Hazard Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Huaker at Huaker-magnetic-balls@outlook.com for assistance.

Products.Name: Huaker Magnetic Balls and Rods Set

Images.Caption: Recalled Huaker Magnetic Balls and Rods Set
Recalled Huaker Magnetic Balls and Rods Set

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from September 2025 to November 2025 for approximately $23.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Huakechuang Technology Co. Ltd., dba Huaker, of China

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to small balls in the toy set

Remedies.Name: Stop using the sets immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Huaker for a full refund. Dispose of the sets and send a photo of the disposed items to Huaker-magnetic-balls@outlook.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Huaker recalls Magnetic Balls and Rods sets due to choking hazards. Contact Huaker for refunds and disposal instructions.

recalls.Description: Vndueey is recalling its Magnetic Men toy sets due to safety concerns regarding loose magnets that can pose a serious ingestion hazard for children. Each set includes 10 flexible stick figures in various colors, each equipped with four small magnets located in their hands and feet. If swallowed, these magnets can attract each other or other metal objects, potentially leading to severe intestinal injuries or even death.
recalls.Title: Vndueey Magnetic Men Toy Sets Recalled Due to Serious Injury Risk from Magnet Ingestion
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Vndueey via email at vndueeyrecall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Vndueey Magnetic Men Toy Set-Multicolor
Each stick figure in the recalled Vndueey toy set has four small magnets, one in each hand and foot

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from June 2025 to August 2025 for approximately $10.

Distributors.Name: Shanghai Zhidi Network Technology Co., Ltd., dba Vndueey, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of ingestion due to loose magnets in the toy sets

Remedies.Name: Stop using the toy sets immediately, keep them away from children, and request a full refund from Vndueey. Consumers must cut the figures in half, send a photo of the destroyed toys to vndueeyrecall@outlook.com, and then dispose of the remains.

recalls.metaDescription: Vndueey recalls Magnetic Men toy sets due to ingestion risks. Immediate action required for consumer safety. Contact for refunds.

recalls.Description: Based Online is recalling its Fidget Magnet Ball Toy Sets due to safety concerns. Each set contains four textured, silicone-covered magnet balls in colors including white, yellow, blue, and purple, each measuring approximately 1.24 inches in diameter. These toys are packaged in a black fabric zippered case and lack identifying marks.
recalls.Title: Recall of Based Online Fidget Magnet Ball Toys Due to Serious Ingestion Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Based Online at recall@mail.goclimber.cn.

Products.Name: Based Online Fidget Magnet Ball Toy Set

Images.Caption: Recalled Based Online Fidget Magnet Ball Toys

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from February 2025 to October 2025 for around $6.

Distributors.Name: Xiaofei He, dba Based Online, of China

Hazards.Name: The toys contain loose high-powered magnets that pose a serious ingestion risk to children. If swallowed, these magnets can attract each other or other metal objects, potentially leading to severe digestive issues, including perforations, blockages, blood poisoning, and even death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled toys immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Based Online for a full refund. It is advised to dispose of the toys and send a photo of the disposal to Based Online at recall@mail.goclimber.cn.

recalls.metaDescription: Based Online recalls Fidget Magnet Ball Toys due to ingestion hazards. Immediate action required for consumer safety.

recalls.Description: Anzmtosn is recalling its Magnet Fidget Spinner Sets due to safety concerns. Each set includes 15 items: four iridescent oval magnets, eight cuboidal magnetic stones, a red and yellow disk-shaped fidget spinner, a winged metal fidget spinner, and a multicolored infinity fidget cube, all packaged in a black fabric-lined storage case. The batch number CCA06582 can be found on the back label of the packaging.
recalls.Title: Anzmtosn Magnet Fidget Spinner Sets Recalled Due to Severe Injury Risk from Magnet Ingestion
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Anzmtosn via email at Anzmtosn53@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Anzmtosn Magnet Fidget Spinner Set
Recalled Anzmtosn Magnet Fidget Spinner Set, Back Label with Batch Numbe

Manufacturers.Name: Shenzhenshibangduoladianzikejimaoyi Co., Ltd. (Shenzhen Bangduola Electronic Technology Trade Co., Ltd.), of China

Retailers.Name: Available through Spartan dealers across the country from February 2025 to October 2025, priced between $16,400 and $19,700.

Hazards.Name: Risk of ingestion due to loose magnets, which can lead to serious health complications such as intestinal blockage or perforation.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must immediately stop using the recalled sets, keep them away from children, and dispose of them. To obtain a full refund, send a photo of the disposed items to Anzmtosn at Anzmtosn53@163.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert: Anzmtosn Magnet Fidget Spinner Sets pose serious ingestion risks. Learn how to safely dispose and claim your refund.

recalls.Description: The recall pertains to Generic magnetic ball sets, which consist of 1,000 multi-colored 3 mm (0.1 inch) magnetic balls. These magnets are packaged in clear plastic and labeled with "1000 Magnets" and "3MM" on the front. Due to their strong magnetic flux, they pose a significant risk if ingested, particularly for children. The presence of loose magnets violates safety standards for toys.
recalls.Title: Recall of Generic Magnetic Ball Sets Due to Severe Injury Risk from Magnet Ingestion; Sold by Ritons on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Ritons via email at recall0817@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Generic Magnetic Balls
Recalled Generic Magnetic Balls

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from November 2024 to August 2025 for approximately $35.

Distributors.Name: Yiwuyichengmaoyiyouxiangongsi, of China, doing business as Amazon seller Ritons

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to loose magnets that can cause serious internal injuries or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must immediately stop using the recalled sets, keep them away from children, and reach out to Ritons for refund instructions. A prepaid return label will be provided.

recalls.metaDescription: Generic magnetic ball sets recalled due to ingestion risks; contact Ritons for refunds and safety instructions.

recalls.Description: Tegu is recalling its Magnetic Floating Stackers, specifically model numbers STA-BGY-801T (Rainbow) and STA-BTP-806T (Big Top). These wooden toys feature a base, six colorful stacking pieces, two posts, and two figures that connect using magnets. Notably, the products lack any identifying markings. The recall is prompted by safety concerns regarding the magnets that can detach, posing a serious ingestion risk to children. Ingested magnets can attract to each other or metal objects, leading to severe health issues such as intestinal blockages and potential fatalities.
recalls.Title: Tegu Recalls Magnetic Floating Stackers Toys Due to Ingestion Risk and Safety Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should immediately remove the product from children's access and reach out to Tegu at 877-Tegu-Toy (877-834-8869) from 10 a.m. to 7 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email service@tegu.com or visit https://tegu.com/pages/recall for further details.

Products.Name: Magnetic Floating Stackers (Rainbow)
Products.NumberOfUnits: 22,850 (In addition, about 1,977 in Canada and about 190 in Mexico)

Images.Caption: Recalled Magnetic Floating Stackers toy, model STA-BGY-801T (Rainbow)
Recalled Magnetic Floating Stackers toy, model STA-BTP-806T (Big Top)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 31 reports of magnets separating from the toy stacker. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Tegu Holdings Inc., of Naples, Florida

Retailers.Name: Available at toy, children's, and specialty stores nationwide, as well as online at Tegu.com and Amazon.com from August 2018 through April 2025 for approximately $40.

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to loose magnets that can detach from the toy

Remedies.Name: Free replacement stacker toy upon contacting Tegu

recalls.metaDescription: Tegu recalls Magnetic Floating Stackers due to a serious ingestion hazard. Contact Tegu for a free replacement toy.

recalls.Description: FidgetThings is recalling its Chill Pill Magnetic Haptic Fidget Toys in Red Bumpy due to a significant safety hazard. These toys resemble pill capsules and consist of two magnetic halves: a white base and a red, bumpy top. They were sold in a mock prescription bottle featuring a label that reads "3DPrint Bumpy." The toys measure approximately two inches, while the bottle is about three inches tall. Due to the nature of the strong magnets, there is a risk of ingestion, which can lead to severe health complications in children.
recalls.Title: FidgetThings Recalls Chill Pill Magnetic Haptic Fidget Toys Due to Serious Ingestion Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers should contact FidgetThings via email at fidgetthings@gmail.com or visit www.fidgetthings.com/pages/magnet-safety. Alternatively, visit www.fidgetthings.com and click on "Magnet Safety" at the bottom of the homepage.

Products.Name: Chill Pill Magnetic Haptic Fidget Toy - Red Bumpy

Images.Caption: Recalled FidgetThings Chill Pill Magnetic Haptic Fidget Toy - Red Bumpy

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at www.FidgetThings.com from October 2022 to February 2025 for approximately $10.

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to violation of federal regulations for magnets

Remedies.Name: Stop using the toy immediately and keep it away from children. Contact FidgetThings at fidgetthings@gmail.com for a pre-paid return label or disposal instructions to receive a full refund. The company is also reaching out to known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: FidgetThings recalls Chill Pill Magnetic Fidget Toys due to serious ingestion risks. Learn how to return for a full refund.

recalls.Description:
Fat Brain Toy Co. is recalling individual PlayTab Rollers Tile toys that were intended for use with the PlayTab Modular Activity Board. These toys were available individually or as part of the PlayTab Sensory Set 3, featuring colorful rubberized rollers designed for tactile play. However, a serious safety hazard has been identified due to the potential for magnets to become loose and pose a risk of ingestion by children.

If ingested, these high-powered magnets can attract each other or other metal objects, leading to severe health complications such as intestinal blockages, infections, or even death. The company is taking this action to ensure the safety of children and comply with federal regulations regarding toy magnets.
recalls.Title: Fat Brain Recalls PlayTab Rollers Tile Toys Due to Magnet Ingestion Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach out to Fat Brain Toy Co. at 800-590-5987 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or via email at recall@fatbraintoys.com. More details are available online at https://fatbraintoys.com/info/toy_safety/play_tab_roller_tile_recall.cfm or by visiting fatbraintoys.com and clicking on the 'Recall' section.

Products.Name: PlayTab Rollers Tile Toys
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 5,100 (In addition, about 380 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Individual Rollers Tile
Recalled Rollers Tile included in PlayTab Sensory Set 3

Retailers.Name: Available at specialty toy, book, and educational stores nationwide, as well as online at fatbraintoys.com from July 2024 through November 2024. Individual rollers tiles were priced around $6, while the Sensory Set 3 retailed for approximately $18.

Importers.Name: Fat Brain Toy Co. LLC, of Elkhorn, Nebraska

Hazards.Name: Risk of ingestion due to loose magnets, which can cause severe internal injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled toys and contact Fat Brain Toy Co. for disposal instructions. They can receive a $10 credit for fatbraintoys.com or a replacement PlayTab tile.

recalls.metaDescription: Fat Brain recalls PlayTab Rollers Tile Toys due to a serious ingestion hazard. Learn how to return your product for a credit or replacement.

recalls.Description: JinJiang Baimei is recalling its Multifunctional Puzzle Crab Toys, which include a magnetic fish hook, eight magnetic pieces, four plastic puzzle pieces, and a plastic mallet. The model number "HY-99" can be found on the bottom of the packaging. These colorful sets are identified by the label "Multifunctional Puzzle" on both the front and back of the box.
recalls.Title: Recall of Multifunctional Puzzle Crab Toys Due to Serious Ingestion Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to JinJiang Baimei Co. Ltd via email at xmjytc@gmail.com.

Importers.Name: Lucky Bird Inc., of Buffalo, New York

Distributors.Name: JinJiang Baimei Co. Ltd, of China

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to violation of federal regulations for toy magnets

Remedies.Name: Stop using the toy immediately, keep it away from children, and contact JinJiang Baimei for a pre-paid return label to receive a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: JinJiang Baimei recalls Multifunctional Puzzle Crab Toys due to serious ingestion hazards. Learn how to return for a refund.

recalls.Description: Round 2 has issued a recall for specific models of Auto World Deluxe Pit Kit slot cars due to a potential ingestion hazard. These slot cars are sold unassembled, with some models available painted or unpainted. Each package features the 'Auto World' logo and the model name, with assembly instructions on the back. The recalled models contain two loose magnets that are not secured to the chassis before assembly, posing a risk to children if ingested. Once assembled, the magnets are attached and do not present this danger.
recalls.Title: Recall of Auto World Deluxe Pit Kit Slot Cars Due to Ingestion Risk from Loose Magnets
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should keep the recalled slot cars away from children and reach out to Round 2 at 888-770-7120 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, or email deluxepitkit@realtimeresults.net. For further details, visit http://autoworldstore.com/deluxepitkitrecall or go to http://autoworldstore.com and select 'Deluxe Pit Kit Recall'.

Products.Name: Auto World Deluxe Pit Kit Slot Cars

Retailers.Name: Available online at autoworldstore.com, protinkertoys.com, and hobbylobby.com, as well as in various retail locations including Hobby Lobby, SlotsnStuff, NitroHobbies Inc., and more. These products were sold from July 2023 through February 2024 for approximately $22.

Importers.Name: Round 2 LLC, of South Bend, Indiana

Hazards.Name: The unassembled Deluxe Pit Kit slot cars contain loose magnets that can pose an ingestion risk to children, violating federal regulations. If swallowed, these magnets may attract each other or other metal objects, leading to serious health issues such as intestinal blockage, perforation, infection, or even death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to keep the recalled slot cars away from children and contact Round 2 for a pre-paid return label to receive a full refund. Round 2 will directly notify all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: Auto World recalls Deluxe Pit Kit slot cars due to ingestion hazards from loose magnets. Contact Round 2 for refunds and safety information.

recalls.Description:
The 6-in-1 Pounding Game, featuring a wooden frame with a frog clock, colorful gears, and animal-faced pegs, is being recalled due to safety concerns. This game includes wooden carrots and worms with metal heads, as well as two mallets and a wooden bird that contains a strong magnet. The magnet can detach, posing a serious ingestion hazard for children. If swallowed, the magnet can attract other metallic objects, leading to severe health risks such as intestinal blockage or perforation.

Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled game immediately and keep it away from children. To obtain a full refund, dispose of the game safely and take a photo of the disposal. This photo should be sent to DMITOY via email or through a message on Temu.com. DMITOY is reaching out to all known buyers to inform them of this recall.
recalls.Title: DMITOY Recalls 6-in-1 Pounding Game Due to Ingestion Risk from Loose Magnet
recalls.ConsumerContact: DMITOY via email at dmitoy202405@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled 6-in-1 Pounding Game Sold by Dmitoy
Recalled 6-in-1 Pounding Game Sold by Dmitoy (Front of Box)
Recalled 6-in-1 Pounding Game Sold by Dmitoy (Game Contents)

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Temu.com from May 2024 to July 2024 for approximately $20.

Hazards.Name: Ingestion risk from loose magnet

Remedies.Name: Stop using the game and dispose of it for a full refund

recalls.metaDescription: DMITOY recalls the 6-in-1 Pounding Game due to a loose magnet posing a serious ingestion risk to children. Immediate action required.

recalls.Description: Elongdi has issued a recall for their multicolored magnetic building sticks sets, which include 130 pieces made of plastic and metal. Each set features 100 magnetic sticks and 30 steel balls, packaged in a box displaying the "Elongdi" logo along with the words "Magnetic Sticks". The item number for this product is E107. This recall is prompted by safety concerns regarding ingestion hazards associated with high-powered magnets.
recalls.Title: Elongdi Magnetic Building Sticks Sets Recalled Due to Ingestion Risk and Regulatory Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Elongdi at 202-375-0895 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at support@elongdi.net.

Images.Caption: Recalled Magnetic Building Sticks Set, 130 Pieces with 100 Magnetic Sticks
Packaging of the Recalled Magnetic Sticks Set (Front of Box)
Packaging of the Recalled Magnetic Sticks Set (Back of Box)

Injuries.Name: None reported. CPSC estimates 2,400 magnet ingestions were treated in hospital emergency departments from 2017 through 2021. CPSC is aware of eight deaths since 2005 through 2021 involving the ingestion of hazardous magnets, including two outside of the United States.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Amazon.com from March 2024 to June 2024 for approximately $20.

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to high-powered magnets that can cause serious internal injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are urged to immediately stop using the recalled sets, keep them away from children, and contact Elongdi for a pre-paid return label to obtain a full refund. Amazon will directly notify all known buyers.

recalls.metaDescription: Elongdi recalls magnetic building sticks sets due to ingestion hazards. Contact for refunds and safety information.

recalls.Description: A recall has been issued for magnetic chess games that include 20 small, spherical magnet stones, which pose a serious ingestion hazard. These games are packaged in a blue box labeled with terms like "Magnetic", "Magnetism", or "Magnetism Intelligence Strategy Game". Each set contains approximately 20 loose magnets, a string, a plastic storage tray lined with sponge, and gameplay instructions.
recalls.Title: Recall of Magnetic Chess Games Due to Ingestion Risk; Noncompliance with Federal Toy Magnet Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Outad Good Life via email at pdhnfgjg@163.com, send a message through the "message" icon on Temu in the seller profile, or check "Your Recalls and Product Safety Alerts" at www.temu.com.

Injuries.Name: None reported. CPSC estimates 2,400 magnet ingestions were treated in hospital emergency departments from 2017 through 2021. CPSC is aware of seven deaths since 2005 involving the ingestion of hazardous magnets, including two outside of the United States.

Distributors.Name: Outad Good Life Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to small, strong magnets that can cause serious internal injuries if swallowed.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the recalled games immediately, keep them away from children, and reach out to Outad Good Life for a full refund. They can also access their Temu account, locate the order in "Your Orders", and click the "Return/Refund" button. Detailed return instructions are available on the Return and Refund Policy page at Temu.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of magnetic chess games due to ingestion risks. Contact Outad Good Life for refunds and safety instructions.

recalls.Description: Stateside Bead Supply is recalling its 5mm HIGH POWER AAA Grade Magnetic Beads, which are small, spherical magnets with a strong magnetic pull. These beads were sold in strands within clear plastic bags labeled with the company's name. The magnets are gun metal gray and pose a significant risk if ingested. The recall is due to a violation of federal safety regulations concerning magnets, as these beads can fit into small parts and possess excessive strength.
recalls.Title: Stateside Bead Supply Recalls High-Powered 5mm Magnetic Beads Due to Serious Ingestion Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Stateside Bead Supply via email at info@statesidebeadsupply.com or call their toll-free number at 888-644-3448, available from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday to Friday. More details can be found at https://www.statesidebeadsupply.com/recall-notice.html or by visiting www.statesidebeadsupply.com and selecting 'Recall Notice.'

recalls.Description: Getallfun is recalling its 216-piece sets of 5mm magnetic balls due to serious ingestion hazards. These small, spherical neodymium magnets have a strong magnetic force and can pose significant risks if swallowed. The magnets, sold in a clear plastic case and portable tin storage box, do not meet federal safety regulations for toy magnet sets, as they can fit into small parts and exceed strength limits. Ingesting these magnets can lead to severe health issues, including intestinal perforations and infections.
recalls.Title: Getallfun Recalls 216-Piece 5mm Magnetic Ball Sets Due to Ingestion Risks and Safety Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, reach out to Getallfun via email at info@getallfun.com, or visit their website at https://getallfun.com/pages/product-recall. You can also click on 'Product Recall' at the top of their homepage.

Injuries.Name: None reported. CPSC estimates 2,400 magnet ingestions were treated in hospital emergency departments from 2017 through 2021. CPSC is aware of seven deaths involving the ingestion of hazardous magnets, including two outside of the United States.

Retailers.Name: Available at Getallfun's store in Aurora, Illinois, and online at Getallfun.com from May 2021 through October 2023 for approximately $20.

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazards due to non-compliance with federal safety regulations

Remedies.Name: Stop using the magnets immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Getallfun for a pre-paid return label to receive a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Getallfun recalls 216-piece 5mm magnetic ball sets due to ingestion hazards. Learn about the risks and how to get a refund.

recalls.Description:
The recall pertains to 5mm magnetic ball sets, which include small, round neodymium magnets known for their strong magnetic properties. These sets contain 226 colorful magnetic balls packaged in a clear plastic case along with a portable tin storage box and a black storage bag. Due to their size and strength, these magnets pose a significant ingestion hazard, especially for children.

Ingesting these powerful magnets can lead to severe health issues, including intestinal perforations, twisting, blockages, infections, and even death. This recall is a precautionary measure to ensure consumer safety and compliance with federal regulations regarding toy magnets.
recalls.Title: Recall of High-Powered 5mm Magnetic Ball Sets Due to Serious Ingestion Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Joybuy Marketplace Express at 302-426-4543 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, via email at oversea-service@jd.com, or visit https://www.walmart.com/seller/16214.

recalls.Description:
XpressGoods has issued a recall for their 5mm Neodymium Magnetic Balls, which are small, spherical magnets known for their strong magnetic properties. These colorful magnets were sold in sets and packaged in a clear plastic case or a tin storage box. Due to safety concerns, these products do not meet federal toy safety regulations, posing a serious ingestion hazard to children.

When swallowed, these powerful magnets can attract each other or other metal objects, leading to severe health risks such as intestinal blockages, perforations, infections, and even death. It is crucial for consumers to take immediate action regarding this recall.
recalls.Title: XpressGoods Recalls Colorful Neodymium Magnetic Balls Due to Ingestion Risk and Safety Regulation Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact XpressGoods via their website at xpressgoods.com, email support@xpressgoods.com, or visit https://xpressgoods.com/product-recalls/ and select 'Recall Notice'.

Images.Caption: Recalled Colorful Metal Neodymium Magic Magnetic Balls - 8 color, 5mm

Injuries.Name: None reported. CPSC estimates 2,400 magnet ingestions were treated in hospital emergency departments from 2017 through 2021. CPSC is aware of seven deaths involving the ingestion of hazardous magnets, including two outside of the United States.

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.xpressgoods.com from July 2021 to May 2022 for approximately $30.

Hazards.Name: Ingestion Hazard due to Non-compliance with Federal Safety Regulations

Remedies.Name: Stop using the magnets immediately, keep them away from children, and contact XpressGoods for a prepaid return label to receive a full refund or store credit.
